title: unicode: N attribute + finite hard-match
manager: A machine can declare `unicode: N` to fix exactly which Unicode version its string semantics use and to hard-match a finite alphabet, turning "strings over these characters" into a closed, checkable set. This is what lets a machine that processes text still make finiteness claims, and what keeps its meaning stable across the decades Unicode will keep changing out from under it.
cs: `unicode: N` machine attribute pins the Unicode version; enables finite hard-match over a declared alphabet; feeds the finite band and the charset/unicode-rampage umbrella (fsl#1199/#1379).
